What the mechanic does: Each Make/Challenge/Resolve beat records a note: on advance, what it did and what the next worker needs to know; on reject, why it bounced. The note is threaded into the next beat's dispatch so the loop carries its own rationale forward.
Predecessor behavior: reason + message threaded to the next hat via renderPriorHandoff
darkrun detail: domain.rs UnitIteration.note; units.rs record_iteration; manufacture.md handoff block
Rationale / next step: Shipped this session.

What the mechanic does: Stamps when a beat actually finished (not just when it started), for audit timing and ordering of the pass loop.
Rationale / next step: Shipped.

What the mechanic does: The pass count and the active worker are computed from the iteration array rather than stored as separate fields, so they can never drift out of sync with the actual history.
Rationale / next step: Shipped — single source of truth.

What the mechanic does: Every feedback records where it came from — adversarial review, run review, reflection, discovery, drift, operator, annotation, or external — so handling can route by source and the operator/reflection can tell a drift alarm from a reviewer's catch.
Rationale / next step: Shipped (8 variants).

What the mechanic does: When a fix closes a finding, the closer records what it actually did to resolve it; that reply is surfaced to whoever raised the finding instead of a silent status flip.
Rationale / next step: done

What the mechanic does: A finding lists the review/approval stamps it undercuts; closing the finding re-opens exactly those stamps on the target station's units, so the gate re-fires and re-signs against the fixed work.
Rationale / next step: Shipped — a close re-fires the gate.

What the mechanic does: Every resolved tick appends one line to action-log.jsonl (time, track, action, station) — an append-only audit trail the operator and the reflection pass read to see how a run actually walked.
Rationale / next step: Shipped. A per-stage decisions.jsonl is optional later.

What the mechanic does: At station completion the engine hashes each unit's declared input files — the premises it was built on — and re-checks them every tick. An upstream input that moved files Input drift so the station reconsiders work resting on a stale premise.
Rationale / next step: Shipped.

What the mechanic does: On detecting drift the predecessor restamped the witness to the new hash before filing, so the same drift couldn't re-fire. darkrun instead self-heals when the artifact is reverted and re-witnesses only via an explicit accept() — auto-restamp would erase the revert-self-heal path.
Predecessor behavior: restamp before filing so it can't re-fire
Rationale / next step: Deliberate — darkrun's revert-self-heal + accept() is the stronger model here. Don't build.

What the mechanic does: The same drifted artifact files exactly one drift entry rather than one per sweep — the drift id is keyed per artifact (and per unit+input for premises), so re-sweeps overwrite rather than pile up.
Rationale / next step: done

What the mechanic does: Caps how many open drift entries a sweep will file (default 10, env-tunable) so one widely-consumed artifact moving — or a directory rename — can't bury the run in drift the operator can't act on.
Rationale / next step: Shipped.

What the mechanic does: The engine mints a one-time token when it dispatches a verifier; the seal/advance tool refuses without it, so an agent can't self-certify a gate it never actually ran.
Rationale / next step: Do alongside D1: bind a nonce to the recorded quality-gate result so the Audit gate can't be self-sealed. Low value until we see a gate gamed.

What the mechanic does: A sidecar checksum of protected state, for harnesses that run without hooks, to detect out-of-band tampering. darkrun covers the same threat with the guard-workflow-fields + stamp-agent-write hooks.
Rationale / next step: Deliberate — the hook suite already covers hookless tamper. Don't build.

What the mechanic does: Marks a unit dispatched-at when it goes to a subagent; a stale lease (the subagent died) is recovered so the unit re-dispatches instead of hanging. darkrun re-dispatches Pending units automatically and the deadlock halt catches an abandoned in-flight unit.
Rationale / next step: Revisit only if a real crash leaves a wedge the deadlock halt misses — the model already recovers.

What the mechanic does: Detects when the cursor returns the same action with no progress (4x) or churns between two actions (A-B over 8+ ticks) and halts to an Escalate action instead of spinning forever.
Rationale / next step: Already present before this session.

What the mechanic does: A fix-worker repairs code routed back from a checkpoint. Today it edits the shared station worktree/branch directly — the change is baked into the station branch with no clean rollback or conflict pause. Isolating each fix on its own branch + worktree (like station work) gives clean retry, a conflict-pause, and a guarded merge-back.
Predecessor behavior: fix-chain on isolated branch haiku/<slug>/fix-<FB>; merge-back on close
darkrun detail: position.rs FixFeedback runs in the station worktree; lifecycle.rs has the per-station model to mirror
Rationale / next step: You're right — same isolation value as units/stations, for the same reason. NOTE: today isolation is per-STATION, not per-unit — worth confirming whether you want per-unit too.

What the mechanic does: In collaborative modes the Spec phase exposes the set of unmet 'involve the operator' signals — have the conversation, verify it, decompose, verify the decomposition — and holds until they're met. It is backpressure that stops the agent skipping the human; relaxed under autopilot.
Predecessor behavior: one action lists every unmet signal; mode-gated
darkrun detail: dedicated `collaborative` mode hard-holds Spec until darkrun_elaborate_seal; prompt-level backpressure in it
Rationale / next step: Shipped — gated to an explicit collaborative mode so continuous stays linear (operator decision).

What the mechanic does: A stage could be offered to the operator keep-or-drop on first arrival. darkrun's six FSSBPH positions are a fixed invariant — the whole orientation refactor rests on it — and right-sizing collapses them at run start instead of a live gate.
Rationale / next step: Deliberate — the fixed six are the point. Don't build.

What the mechanic does: The feedback track resolves the single most-urgent open item: questions preempt (they need an operator decision before any fix), then fix items by severity (blocker before high before nit), stable by id within a rank — so a blocker is never starved behind an earlier nit.
Rationale / next step: Ordering shipped. Batching/escalation can follow if waves get noisy.

What the mechanic does: Reviews and quality gates that judge the WHOLE run after the last station closes — cross-station consistency, integration, the integrated result — distinct from per-station reviews. darkrun declares run reviewers in content but the walk only gates per-station.
darkrun detail: RunReview holds after the last station until run reviewers stamp via darkrun_run_review_stamp
Rationale / next step: Shipped — the whole-run cross-station audit gates before seal.

What the mechanic does: The run mode (continuous / discrete / autopilot / quick) shapes which review/approval roles fire — at both station and run level — while always keeping the final operator gate. darkrun shapes station-level roles only.
darkrun detail: derive.rs trims station user gate under autopilot
Rationale / next step: Bundle with C4 (run-level gate).

What the mechanic does: Before sealing a run, re-audit that the work actually merged/delivered (PR merged) rather than just holding for a decision. darkrun's discrete gate already polls PR merge; a non-discrete run has no PR to re-audit.
darkrun detail: discrete gate polls PR; PendingSeal otherwise holds
Rationale / next step: Mostly redundant for non-discrete runs. Skip unless a non-discrete delivery path appears.

What the mechanic does: A unit declares its quality gates (test/lint/type commands); the agent runs them (it has a shell) and records each result; the station cannot reach Audit until every declared gate is pass or deferred-to-CI; a repeatedly env-blocked gate auto-defers so it can't wedge the run.
darkrun detail: domain.rs QualityGate/GateResult; units.rs record_gate_result; position.rs gates_unmet gate
Rationale / next step: Shipped — a passed Audit is now backed by recorded gate results.

What the mechanic does: A unit that declared an output but never wrote the file to disk is held before Audit until the artifact actually exists — a station can't advance on a promised-but-absent output.
Rationale / next step: Shipped.

What the mechanic does: A unit whose declared input names a sibling unit instead of a file path is held — inputs are witnessable file premises, not unit references; the slug belongs in depends_on.
Rationale / next step: Shipped.

What the mechanic does: Ensures each station actually consumes the prior stations' distilled outputs rather than working independently and re-inventing the run. The engine checks that upstream locked artifacts are carried forward as a station's inputs; an output intentionally not carried forward is explicitly acknowledged (out-of-scope / covered-by-unit) rather than silently dropped.
Predecessor behavior: cumulative input coverage + out-of-scope / covered-by-unit acks
darkrun detail: validate_input_coverage: every upstream artifact in inputs or inputs_waived
Rationale / next step: Shipped — no silent loss of distillation; software prove waives frame.md/design.md.

What the mechanic does: Attach the objective proof artifact (vitals, bench numbers, screenshots) to the change request as a durable, linkable asset. darkrun stores and surface-checks proof locally; pushing to a PR is a hosting integration.
Predecessor behavior: durable asset on the change request
darkrun detail: proof.rs stores + block_matches_surface; no PR upload
Rationale / next step: Hosting integration — orthogonal to engine correctness. Do when the PR/MR path matures.

What the mechanic does: A role can declare its own model; the engine resolves it (role override, else factory default) and dispatches that role's beat on the resolved model.
Rationale / next step: Shipped.

What the mechanic does: A reviewer declares its dispute posture — strict (adversarial, find every flaw) or lens (one constructive perspective) — and the review prompt frames that reviewer's pass accordingly.
Rationale / next step: Shipped — build's correctness reviewer is now strict.

What the mechanic does: A worker declares whether it plans, builds, or verifies. On a reject the loop bounces to the nearest preceding BUILD worker (the one that can actually fix), skipping verify-only roles instead of restarting from the top.
Predecessor behavior: rejects bounce to the nearest build hat
darkrun detail: RoleFrontmatter.role; StationDef.worker_roles; manufacture prompt routes the reject-bounce to the nearest build worker via unit_iterate next_worker
Rationale / next step: Shipped — worker role + bounce guidance, composing with the iteration model.

What the mechanic does: A per-worker flag telling that worker to run + record gates during its beat. Now redundant: D1's Audit gate already forces every declared gate to be recorded before the station advances.
Rationale / next step: Build only if a worker needs to gate mid-loop rather than at Audit.

What the mechanic does: Declare a station's inputs as {stage, output} pairs — and pull in reviewers from sibling stages — instead of a flat list of path strings, so cross-station wiring is explicit and checkable.
Rationale / next step: Content-model change with some churn. Moderate.

What the mechanic does: A reviewer runs only on stations producing matching artifact types (e.g. an a11y reviewer only on *.html / *.tsx) rather than firing on every station.
darkrun detail: reviewers are listed per-station in the roster
Rationale / next step: Redundant in darkrun's model — reviewers are per-station rosters, so you scope by simply not listing a reviewer on stations it doesn't apply to. applies_to only helps factory-global auto-applied reviewers, which darkrun doesn't have.

What the mechanic does: A station offers a choice of checkpoint paths (e.g. [external, ask]) and the operator picks at the gate, instead of a single fixed checkpoint kind baked into the station.
Rationale / next step: do
Your comment: Needs a picker at the gate. Moderate.

What the mechanic does: Hooks block raw Read/Write/Edit on engine-managed files and redirect the agent to the proper tools; the full 8-hook suite also stamps writes, monitors context, redirects plan mode, and injects state.
Rationale / next step: Already present.

What the mechanic does: A guarded conversational write tool (allow/deny-list + symlink-escape guard + audit stamp) for when the operator asks the agent to save a file on their behalf. No such flow exists in darkrun yet.
Rationale / next step: Build when a 'save this to knowledge/ for me' UX appears.

What the mechanic does: A PostToolUse hook attributes every agent file edit into a log the drift track reads, so out-of-band edits are witnessed.
Rationale / next step: Already present.

What the mechanic does: A subagent records its own verdict for one review/approval role (and an explorer records its finding) WITHOUT walking the cursor, so N reviewers/explorers fan out in parallel, each writes its own result, and the parent ticks once — no contention, no deadlock-guard trip.
Predecessor behavior: review_stamp stamps one role without a cursor walk
darkrun detail: darkrun_review_stamp records one role without a cursor walk; Review/Audit dispatch in parallel
Rationale / next step: Shipped — parallel reviewers each self-stamp; parent ticks once. (Explorers already fan out + fold into the spec.)

What the mechanic does: Track client presence with attached-then-lost and never-attached grace windows plus reattach precedence, so a backgrounded review tab doesn't look disconnected. darkrun has live-connection counting + a heartbeat but no grace windows.
Rationale / next step: Polish for a remote-review path that isn't here yet.

What the mechanic does: Stamp the engine/plugin version a run was created with (immutable) and migrate older on-disk shapes forward on read. Cheap to start stamping before more on-disk shapes ship.
Rationale / next step: Worth stamping the version early; migrators later.

What the mechanic does: Hold cross-system handles (ticket key, PR url, design link) on the run/unit as durable pointers between darkrun and external systems.
Rationale / next step: Additive field — add when integrations land.

What the mechanic does: A stage-keyed store of operator Q&A answers. darkrun already captures this in the run doc / annotations, so a separate store is redundant.
Rationale / next step: Redundant with existing capture.

What the mechanic does: Track a PR's status (draft -> ready -> merged, with ready_at) rather than just its ref, so the engine knows where the change request stands. darkrun stores the bare pr_ref.
Rationale / next step: Extend pr_ref with a status field when the discrete path matures.

What the mechanic does: Persist interactive session metadata so it survives an engine restart. darkrun keeps sessions in-memory by design — the on-disk run state is the durable truth — so persisting buys little.
Rationale / next step: Persist only if restart-survival becomes a hard requirement.
